page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs, with a single thread per core configuration. The memory allocation consists of 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and utilizing a KVM hypervisor. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results position this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a stress-ng single-core score of 1575.58 and a multi-core score of 12474.34. Memory bandwidth tests show 698.22 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 55.6 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. However, the instance exhibits performance trade-offs in specific workloads, with a weak PassMark memory score of 1777.75 and a weak PassMark CPU physics score of 741.78. LLM inference benchmarks also reflect limitations, showing weak performance on medium 7B models compared to average performance on small 135M models.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.cm4.3xlarge offers stable resource density for general-purpose workloads. It achieves average performance in database operations with 3402.78 PassMark database operations per second and 11.64 million Redis SET operations per second. The instance is suitable for static web serving, yielding a throughput of 7.8 GB/sec, as well as light serialization tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. It is best utilized for application servers, microservices, and standard database hosting where local storage and GPU acceleration are not required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12474.34 and a Redis SET throughput of 11.64 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in CPU physics and memory-intensive PassMark tests. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, and lightweight serialization, where dedicated CPU resources are required without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core Intel Xeon instance offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation and stable baseline network performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and physical cores running at 3.2 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
No local storage or GPU hardware included in the configuration
Suited for general-purpose applications, microservices, and database hosting
